description.step_1: Whole numbers are a set of numbers that include zero and all positive integers.

description.step_2: They do not contain any fractional or decimal parts, making them discrete quantities easily represented on a number line with equal intervals between consecutive whole numbers (e.g., 1, 2, 3, ...).

description.step_3: Whole numbers are used in everyday counting and can be grouped into categories like cardinal numbers for quantity, ordinal numbers to indicate order or position, etc.
